# Team Flow — checker rules
_Loaded by `flow-checker` after Serena activation. Edit freely below the imported block._

# imported from CLAUDE.md (auto-regenerated by /teams:init)
- [must-fix] Verify compliance with AGENTS.md §Project Structure & Module Organization, §Coding Style & Naming Conventions, §Agent-Specific Instructions; CLAUDE.md §Codebase Navigation across the full feature.
# end imported

## Final-check rules
- [must-fix] Re-run the four exit-gate commands on a clean working tree (`npm run build`, `npm run lint`; `test_command` is `none` — skip, do not invent). If uncommitted unrelated changes exist, list them in `check-result.md` and set `Status: HAS_ISSUES`.
- [must-fix] Aggregate metrics in `check-result.md` (line delta, test count) must equal the sum across `phase-*-result.md`. Discrepancies are `must-fix`.
- [must-fix] Verify rule compliance: confirm `.claude/rules/teams-flow/*` rules were applied across phases. Missing application = `HAS_ISSUES`.
- [must-fix] Confirm forbidden zones (`dist/`, `public/`, `index.html`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig*.json`, `.github/workflows/`) were not touched anywhere across the diff (`git diff --stat $BASE..HEAD -- dist/ public/ index.html vite.config.ts 'tsconfig*.json' .github/workflows/`).
- [should-fix] If `phase-*-result.md` files are missing, reconstruct them from review reports + `git diff` before declaring `Status: DONE`.

## Checker-specific tactics
The senior-specialist baseline in `_shared.md` is in force across the full feature. The items below are checker-specific framings; they do not duplicate the baseline.

- [must-fix] Verify the feature solves the **brief**, not just that the gates are green. Trace the user-facing flow described in the brief end-to-end through the code (UI dispatch → reducer → use-case → new `Board` → render → `localStorage` save via `store.subscribe`). If any required step is missing or broken, set `Status: HAS_ISSUES` regardless of phase reports. For UI-visible changes, AGENTS.md §Pull Requests requires screenshots/screen-recordings — note their absence in the report (advisory, not blocking).
- [must-fix] Scope audit: diff the final state against the brief's "Out of scope" list. Any out-of-scope change merged across phases is `HAS_ISSUES`, even if individually beneficial.
- [must-fix] Regression check: identify at least one untouched-but-adjacent feature and confirm it still works via existing test, by tracing call sites of changed symbols (`find_referencing_symbols`), or — since `test_command: none` — by manual verification through `npm run dev`. Specifically, for any change touching `src/infrastructure/persistence/`, manually verify the legacy v1 → v2 migration path still works (DevTools → Application → Local Storage → set the legacy `work-grid-board` key, reload, confirm migration). A green build does not prove the absence of regressions in untested paths.
- [must-fix] Persistence backwards-compatibility audit: for any change to `Workspace` / `Board` shapes, confirm `parseWorkspace` either accepts the old shape unchanged or includes a documented migration path (analogous to `workspaceFromLegacyBoardJson`). If `schemaVersion` was bumped without a migration, set `Status: HAS_ISSUES`.
- [must-fix] Hexagonal-layering audit on the full diff: `grep -rE "from ['\"]\.\.?/.*infrastructure" src/application src/domain` and similar for `features` → `application/domain` only. Any hits = `HAS_ISSUES`.
- [must-fix] Sweep the final diff for senior-baseline violations that slipped past phase reviews — leftover scaffolding (`console.log`, commented-out code, unreferenced exports), defensive shims, speculative abstractions, backwards-compat forwarders, WHAT-comments. Any leak is `HAS_ISSUES`. Use `git diff --unified=0` + `grep` for the scaffolding token list in `_shared.md`.
- [should-fix] If a baseline rule was clearly violated and slipped past review, record the pattern in `check-result.md` under "Process follow-ups" so the team lead can recalibrate the reviewer even when it doesn't block ship.
